query: Marked 3: Writing to LLMs, Marked is the ultimate Markdown preview complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Suggested strategies for "skipping" incomplete/malformed ... - GitHub
results.content:
your responses are unhelpful and do not address the original question

The original question has the problem backwards, and is a non goal for the project.  
We parse standard markdown , we're happy to offer advise on how to generate valid markdown, but have no intent to try to handle invalid content in a way that goes against the specifications.

unhelpful, you're just complaining about how LLMs generate markdown

It isn't a complaint so much as an observation.  
At the end of the day, you own your content creation pipeline, if your human/ai/whatever authors generate invalid content. That is a problem of the pipeline, not of markdown.

My suggestions are towards ways that the problem can be solved, which is addressing the issue where it originates, in the pipeline. [...] | your responses are unhelpful and do not address the original question  The original question has the problem backwards, and is a non goal for the project.  We parse standard markdown , we're happy to offer advise on how to generate valid markdown, but have no intent to try to handle invalid content in a way that goes against the specifications.  unhelpful, you're just complaining about how LLMs generate markdown  It isn't a complaint so much as an observation.  At the end of the day, you own your content creation pipeline, if your human/ai/whatever authors generate invalid content. That is a problem of the pipeline, not of markdown.  My suggestions are towards ways that the problem can be solved, which is addressing the issue where it originates, in the pipeline.  the pragmatic solution [...] Option 4, choose a faster model or hosting service.  
Your frustration is with users seeing an incomplete document, all three options above will make a user wait even longer to see a complete document.

I'd be particularly cautious about any library that attempts to match a particular LLM's output.  
Different LLMs have different markdown preferences, even within the same company (OpenAI, Anthopic, Google, Mistral) different models have different preferences, even within the same model class (like ChatGPT 4o), different checkpoints will generated different markdown.  
Trying to match these different and ever changing targets is futile and backwards.

results.title: Marked 3 (Markdown previewer) - Software - MPU Talk
results.content:
Agreed. I bought version 2 less than a month ago and was really a fan but I abandoned it after two weeks and am now using Obsidian instead. My main complaint was that there was no real docking for Marked 2 with BBEdit but I simulated some automatic side-by-side placement logic using Keyboard Maestro with some Applescript. I think it’s a tad overpriced myself (for my purposes) but I’m sure that it’s worth it to the right kind of user who will take advantage of the features. I would have liked the Scrivener feature but I abandoned that program months ago. I had lots of docs in Pages so I like a conversion utility for that but I don’t remember that being part of the feature set - and I’ve converted those manually myself, I’d sill like to have Marked 3 though for the Schema ripping feature. [...] MPU Talk

# Marked 3 (Markdown previewer)

Brett Terpstra did a soft launch of Marked 3 today:

### Marked 3 (@marked@indieapps.space)

Marked 3 is officially available on all channels, though I'm holding off on a big post/newsletter about it until the next version gets approved in the MAS with bugfixes. But you can download from the Mac App Store, Setapp, or direct from...

### Marked - The Ultimate Markdown Preview and Converter

Marked is a powerful markdown preview and converter. It supports HTML, PDF, DOCX, and more.

On macOS $30/year or $70 permanent unlock.

For a markdown previewer/exporter – pretty steep.

Katie

I like whatever version I’m using with SetApp (2 I assume) but I can’t imagine paying £30 a year for it. [...] But it does its job well and is even more helpful in this AI era of lots of markdown files. Assuming 3 is an improvement, could well be handy for some.

By the way I combine it with OpenIn so that when I open a md file I can choose to edit it in MarkedIt or view it Marked. Works really well, better than a split view app imo.

Version 3 is in Setapp now and should be automatically updated – at least mine was… I haven’t tried it out yet though.

Version 3 is in Setapp now

:+1:

:+1:

Works fine

Katie

On macOS $30/year or $70 permanent unlock.

For a markdown previewer/exporter – pretty steep.

As a beta tester, I received a 50% discount for the lifetime option, but even €39,99 (the full price is €79,99 in Europe) is still pretty steep… (IMHO)

results.title: The “Broken” Markdown: Why I Built a Specialized LLM-Ready Viewer
results.content:
## The Problem I Faced

If you have ever integrated an LLM API into a backend system or worked with raw JSON responses, you’ve seen the “ugly” version of Markdown generated by LLMssss. While chat interfaces like Gemini or ChatGPT render everything beautifully, the actual data being sent over the wire often looks like this

### Top Recommendations\n\n1.  Python : High versatility.\t- Used for AI.\n2.  Rust : Memory safety.
When you paste that into a standard Markdown editor (like VS Code, Obsidian, or a basic online renderer), it breaks. Standard renderers expect literal newlines (actual hits of the Enter key). They don’t know what to do with `\n` or `\t.` [...] Sitemap

Open in app

Sign up

Sign in

. But there is a silent, friction filled problem that occurs the moment we try to take that AI output and put it into a standard Markdown renderer to preview. Yes, I am talking about he “The Escape Character Nightmare”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image 3

Source: Image by AI

So I gave this problem to the Claude and within 3 mins it built this Markdown Viewer because I was tired of “cleaning” my text before I could actually read it. This isn’t a complex enterprise platform it’s a simple utility built to solve a specific, recurring headache.

## The Problem I Faced [...] I found myself stuck in a loop generating a markdown output from LLM, receiving a string full of escaped characters, and then having to manually “un escape” it just to see if the formatting looked right. It was a classic “papercut” in the workflow small, but painful when it happens fifty times a day.

Mac

Marked is a Markdown Preview application (NOT AN EDITOR) with advanced writing tools and export options. Marked 3 is faster and more powerful than ever! With completely revamped DOCX support, plus new export formats like EPUB and TextBundle, Marked 3 is a valuable tool in your publishing workflow. There's a new Style Manager and Style Editor, Custom Rules for defining different processors for different file types, built-in transforms for things like shifting header levels, replacing text, and working with metadata, and more new features than you can count.
